description: A child pictures a house under the sea; a repairer considers a new joint before cutting the wood. Both use 'weni' to form in the mind what is not presently perceived. 'whemura' (dream) includes sleep dreams and waking visions held as dreams; 'winora' (plan) arranges intended action.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w'. Unround as the tongue moves forward into stressed true-mid front 'e'. For voiced dental 'n', press the tongue tip behind the upper teeth and let air leave through the nose, then finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: The imagined object appears directly before 'weni'. A complete scenario instead uses 'mena ... meno', and direct imperative 'no weni' asks someone to imagine. 'weni' can also be the noun imagination or an act of imagining; 'wiero' (imaginative) supplies the quality.

pillars.solarpunk-values: With 'weni', a community pictures rooftops feeding shared cisterns before any design is chosen. People can discuss the possible arrangement before it becomes a plan.

semantic_domains.cognition: forming mental content beyond what is presently perceived
semantic_domains.creation: imagining a possible work, design, story, or arrangement before or during making
